steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage moves forward in time: Shakespeare arrived in London around 1585, was known by 1591, and the blank introduces what happened in 1596. The relationship between sentences is temporal sequence — one event follows another in time.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In other words,': signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea, but 1596 introduces a new event, not a rephrasing of 1591. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Conversely,': signals direct contrast or opposition; the 1596 event does not oppose or contradict the 1591 one. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Thus,': signals a logical consequence or conclusion drawn from what came before; being known for plays in 1591 does not cause the 1596 performance. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Later,': signals forward movement in time, fitting perfectly with the progression from 1591 to 1596. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
